steps.n: This is a common word used at the start of sentences to add emphasis, like saying 'Listen!' before important news

steps.m: indeed we (subject of أَنزَلْنٰهُ - we sent it down)
steps.n: The long alif (آ) at the end shows this is about 'we/us'. This connects to the next word أَنزَلْنٰهُ (we sent it down) where we are doing the action.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning makes the meaning causative - showing that someone is making the action happen
Added نٰ at the end to show 'we' are doing the action

steps.m: we sent it down (object of إِنَّ)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end meaning 'it'. This word is affected by إِنَّ at the start of the verse.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside something else or happens during a time. In this ayah, it shows that something happened during لَيْلَةِ ٱلْقَدْرِ (the Night of Power).
The ى at the end helps connect this word to what comes after it. Here it connects to 'the Night of Power' to show when something happened.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic word for night
Added ة to specify one particular night

steps.m: of a single night (possessed by القَدْرِ)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this night belongs to or is connected to القَدْرِ (the Night of Power/Decree)
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning the result of determining/measuring
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to the specific power/destiny being discussed

steps.m: of the power/destiny (possessed by ليلة - night)
steps.n: Adding kasra at the end shows this word belongs to or is connected to ليلة (night), making it 'Night of Power'
This is an interrogative particle that asks questions
Added و at the beginning to connect this question with what came before it
Added the elongated alif (آ) at the end to show the extended pronunciation, but this doesn't change the meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Added أَ at beginning and changed vowels to make the verb causative (making someone else do the action)
Added alif maksura (ى with mark) at the end to show proper spelling of weak letter

steps.m: made you know (object of وَمَآ - 'and what')
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show 'you' as the object receiving the action. This word follows the question word 'what' (ما) in the phrase 'and what made you know?'
This is an interrogative particle used to ask questions. In this ayah, it's asking about the Night of Power (لَيْلَةُ ٱلْقَدْرِ).
Added fatha mark to show the correct pronunciation
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make them pronounceable
The ة (taa marbutah) creates a singular noun and specifies one instance/unit
The word gets a special mark (ُ) at the end to show it's the main subject being talked about, like saying 'THE Night' instead of just 'a night'

steps.m: to determine, to measure out, to have power over

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create a noun meaning 'divine power/decree'. This word belongs to لَيْلَةُ (night) in this ayah, forming the phrase 'Night of Power'.
Added ٱلْ to specify this particular divine decree/power

steps.m: of the divine decree/power (possessed by لَيْلَةُ)
steps.n: Added kasra to show this describes لَيْلَةُ (Night), forming 'Night of Power'
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a basic noun
Added ة to show we're talking about one specific night
Added ُ to show this is the subject of the sentence. This Night is being described as better than a thousand months.
Changes verb into noun meaning 'the act/result of measuring'
Added ال to make it specific and ِ to show it belongs to لَيْلَةُ (night)
Added fatha and sukun to make the comparative form, which shows something is more/most of a quality
Added double damma to show this word is the subject in 'The Night of Power is better than a thousand months'
Basic preposition used here to make a comparison, showing that something is better than something else
The م gets doubled (shadda) because it comes after a word ending in ن or م - this is a common rule in Arabic pronunciation
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it pronounceable
Added kasra at the end to show this thousand belongs to/is connected to the word شَهْرٍ (month) that comes after it

steps.m: to be known/visible (like a new moon)

steps.n: Root meaning 'visible' becomes the word for 'month' since months were known by seeing the new moon. The word gets a fatha-sukun pattern to make it a noun.
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a month') and is possessed by the word 'thousand' before it
Adding basic vowels to make Form I past tense
Adding shadda shows the action happens multiple times or gradually

steps.m: it descends gradually (subject: الْمَلٰٓئِكَةُ - the angels)
steps.n: Adding تَ at beginning changes it from 'someone made something descend' to 'something descends on its own'. Shows the angels coming down by themselves.
Changed from past tense (angels descended) to present tense (angels descend) by changing the ending
Changed root 'to have power' into 'angel' by adding the vowels a-a
Added long 'aa' sound to make the word for angel
Changed to broken plural form for 'angels'
Added ال (the) at start and ُ at end to show these angels are doing the action of descending
Changed from action to the thing itself - the spirit or life force
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific spirit - in this case referring to Angel Jibreel
The ُ shows this is doing the action along with the angels (descending)
Added و to connect with المَلٰٓئِكَةُ (the angels) as they descend together
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment
The ها refers back to ليلة القدر (Night of Power) which was mentioned earlier
Changed the root into a noun meaning the actual permission that is given
Added بِ at the beginning to show this permission is being used as a means or instrument. This word connects to رَبِّهِم (their Lord) which comes after it.
The word gets a kasra (i) sound at the end because it comes after بِ (by/with)

steps.m: lord (one who nurtures and cares for others)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this a noun showing who does the nurturing action
The kasra shows this word is connected to what comes after it
Adding هِم shows this belongs to them
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows something comes from or originates from something else

steps.m: from (emphasizing the start of كُلِّ أَمْرٍ - every matter)
steps.n: The shadda (ّ) adds emphasis to show this is the starting point of 'every matter' in this ayah
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word is connected to أَمْرٍ (matter) that comes after it

steps.m: to command/order, to make something happen

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun by adding vowel marks. This makes it refer to the thing being commanded rather than the action of commanding.
Added tanwin (double kasra) at the end to show this is any command/matter in general, not a specific one. This word belongs to كُلِّ (every/all) in this ayah.
Adding ا after the second root letter makes this a noun meaning the complete state of peace. This word is the predicate (describes what something is) in this ayah, telling us what the night is.
Adding tanween (double damma) makes this an indefinite noun, meaning 'a peace' rather than 'the peace'
This is the basic part of the pronoun that shows we're pointing to something
Adding ى makes it specifically feminine, like saying 'she' instead of just 'it'
Adding َ (fatha) shows this is the subject doing something in the sentence. Here it connects to the word سَلٰمٌ (peace) to mean 'it is peace'
This is the basic form of the word that shows an endpoint or limit
Adding the shadda makes the meaning stronger and more definite

steps.m: until (connects to مَطْلَعِ 'rising' in this ayah)
steps.n: The ىٰ at the end shows this word connects to what comes after it. Here it connects to 'the rising of dawn' (مَطْلَعِ ٱلْفَجْرِ)

steps.m: place or time of rising (related to الْفَجْرِ - dawn)
steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning to show where or when something happens. In this ayah, it specifically refers to when dawn rises.
Added kasra at the end because this word comes after حَتَّىٰ (until) in the ayah

steps.m: to burst forth or split open (like dawn breaking)

steps.n: Added vowel marks to transform the root into a noun meaning the time when light breaks through darkness
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific dawn rather than any dawn

steps.m: of the dawn (object of مَطْلَعِ - rising/emergence)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word follows مَطْلَعِ (rising/emergence), meaning 'the rising of the dawn'
